title: Read drop-ins for instantiated template service without an instanced template file
body:
### Feature request description

If I understand the [docs](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#template-files) correctly, you cannot have the generator reads drop-in files (`temp@a.container.d/`) for an instantiated template service (`temp@a.service`) without having an instanced template file (`temp@a.container`). 

It would be useful to be able to have drop-ins for modifying directives of the base template for instance template services (or even the base template service), meaning reading `temp@.container.d` and `temp@a.container.d` when creating `temp@a.service`, without needing to specify a `temp@a.container`. 

### Suggest potential solution

NA

### Have you considered any alternatives?

NA

### Additional context

For example, I want to create a network per container unit, so I want to use a template `net@.network`, but I don't want to have to copy the whole base container file for some specific service i.e. `net@abc.network`; I want to be able to specify drop-in files i.e. `net@abc.network.d/overrid.conf` to override or add directives. Hence I need the generation of the network service to look at `net@.network` and ``net@abc.network.d`.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
